latestIgtvVideos.title: 金沢Vlog - Kanazawa Vlog
latestIgtvVideos.caption:
GoPro only﻿
﻿
﻿
去年の夏にチャリ🚴‍♂️とGoPro📸を持って撮影してきました。﻿
﻿
﻿
金沢は江戸時代の面影が残る地区や、美術館、伝統工芸で知られています。﻿
﻿
伝統的な街並みと、近代的建造物のハイブリッドという贅沢な街です。﻿
﻿
少し散策するだけで、すぐに観光スポットにぶち当たるとんでもない街なので、時間に余裕を持って訪れましょう。﻿
﻿
﻿
————————————————﻿———
﻿
It is known for its Edo period districts, museums, and traditional crafts.﻿
﻿
It is a luxurious city with a hybrid of traditional streets and modern buildings.﻿
﻿
It is said that you will hit a tourist spot immediately after a short walk﻿
It's a ridiculous city, so make sure you have plenty of time to visit.﻿
﻿
——————————————————﻿

latestPosts.caption:
Petra – a place I had always dreamed of visiting at least once in my life.
A temple carved into stone, where time feels frozen and only the wind speaks.

This journey started with sharing the beauty of Japan.
But somewhere along the way, I began chasing beauty around the world.
No matter where I go, I want to keep capturing the moments that move my heart.

人生で一度は訪れてみたかった場所、ペトラ遺跡。
岩をくり抜いてつくられた壮大な神殿、
風の音だけが響く、時間が止まったような空間。

もともとは、日本の魅力を発信するために始めたこのアカウントですが気づけば“世界の美しさ”にも興味を持った。
海を越えても、心が動く瞬間をこれからも切り取りたい。

@expo2025jordan 

#petra #cinematic #traveling #travelphotography #traveller #filmmaker #filmmakerlife #filmmakerslife #cinematicvideo #expo2025jordan

Enoshima glowing gold at sunset, then the Kamakura fireworks taking over the sky right after. 
Summer evenings in Japan really don’t get better than this.

Shot on IPhone 

#enoshima #kamakura #japan #cinematic

I’m proud of my country. 🇯🇵

It’s beautiful in a way that doesn’t end. Four seasons turn through the year, each with its own charm, its own quiet. And in regions whose names most people will never learn, old traditions still breathe. Histories passed hand to hand, all the way to now.

Japan’s sense of beauty has no final shape. It keeps unfolding. Maybe that’s why it holds us the way it does.

I’m not sure I can explain it. I only know I’m grateful, that this land taught me to feel this deeply.

And so it became my purpose: to carry its beauty out into the world.

1.	Kyoto, Ninenzaka
2.	Yamanashi, Mt. Fuji
3.	Tokyo, Sensoji Temple
4.	Niigata, Nagaoka Fireworks
5.	Kyoto, Ine

biography: Fall in love with the world every day.

latestIgtvVideos.title: The French town celebrating its lemons
latestIgtvVideos.caption:
Menton, France is a coastal town that loves its lemons.

It's known for the Fête du Citron, an annual festival celebrating the history and culture of citrus growing in the region.

Massive crowds come to see the festival's fruited floats – each made with more than three tonnes of lemons and oranges.

latestIgtvVideos.title: The Little Calumet River
latestIgtvVideos.caption:
A little-known river on Chicago's South Side was once a vital part of the Underground Railroad. 

A new water trail aims to get people onto the water to dive into its history.

latestIgtvVideos.title: A freedom festival born from slavery
latestIgtvVideos.caption: In the Bahamas, special parades and the culture that surrounds them are known by one word: Junkanoo. For historian Arlene Nash Ferguson, it's been a lifelong passion. To learn more, visit bbc.com/travel #BBCTravel #Junkanoo #TheBahamas #LoveTheWorld

latestIgtvVideos.title: ASMR baking: The cake made with a 280-year-old water mill
latestIgtvVideos.caption: Relax, turn your sound on, and watch this ASMR video of baker Gerard Lhuillier making Gâteau Basque, an emblem of the French Basque Country. You can read more about this on bbc.com/travel. #BBCTravel #ASMR #Baking

latestIgtvVideos.title: The man who mapped a nation by hand
latestIgtvVideos.caption: Known as "the Indiana Jones of Grenada", Telfor Bedeau has kayaked around his Caribbean island home, walked nearly every inch of it and climbed its highest peak 217 times.

latestIgtvVideos.title: The swedish chef who cooks solely with fire
latestIgtvVideos.caption: Evoking a deep a sense of nostalgia, chef Niklas Ekstedt has taken traditional Swedish food to a new level – by cooking fine cuisine over fire without gas or electricity.

latestIgtvVideos.title: Canada's rising Inuit TikTok star
latestIgtvVideos.caption: Inuit throat singing was at risk of extinction after years of erasure by colonists and missionaries, but TikTok star Shina Novalinga is sharing the tradition for a new generation. @shinanova @kayuulanov

latestIgtvVideos.title: Posidonia: the Mediterranean’s ‘super plant’
latestIgtvVideos.caption: 🌱 One of the oldest organisms on earth, the “sea lung,” is the most widespread seagrass in the Mediterranean. 🌊 Named after Poseidon, the Greek god of the sea, this flowering "super plant" is in grave threat of disappearing within the next few decades. In response, Spanish underwater filmmaker and marine biologist Manu San Félix has dedicated his life to saving the Posidonia Oceanica plant. 🌎 #lovetheworld

latestPosts.caption:
From May to July, the high-altitude plateau surrounding the village of Castelluccio di Norcia explode in a rainbow of red, purple, blue and yellow. But the culprit behind this unique floral display isn't a flower – it's the humble lentil.⁣
 ⁣
Tap the link in bio to learn how this annual Italian bloom came to be.⁣
 ⁣
📷: Elisabetta Abrami

Dubbed "the wildest woman in America" due to her snake-rearing, knife-wielding and roadkill-scavenging lifestyle, Carol Ruckdeschel lives off the land and largely off the grid alone in a protected wilderness called Cumberland Island. She fights tenaciously to preserve it for future travellers.⁣
 ⁣
Measuring more than 36,000 acres and located 18 miles (30km) north-east of Jacksonville, Florida, Cumberland is the largest and southernmost of the 14 barrier islands strewn off Georgia's Atlantic coast. ⁣
 ⁣
Ruckdeschel moved to Cumberland in 1973, and for the past 53 years, the ecologist and naturalist has been one of the only full-time residents on one of the Atlantic's most biodiverse barrier islands.⁣
 ⁣
Swipe to see the wild and beautiful remote island, then tap the link in bio to learn more about Ruckdeschel's lifelong fight to preserve it.⁣
 ⁣
📷: Alexandra Marvar, Getty Images

The Economist Intelligence Unit's latest Global Liveability Index assesses 173 cities worldwide across stability, healthcare, culture and environment, education, and infrastructure to identify those offering the highest quality of life – here are their top five picks. ⁣
 ⁣
Swipe to the end to see who landed the #1 spot, then tap the link in bio for the full list.⁣
 ⁣
📷: Getty Images

Welcome to the Ardèche, where signs for homemade goat cheese, chestnuts and honey frequently appear on the roadside.⁣
 ⁣
Due to its unruly mountainous geography and low population density, the Ardèche is the only of France's 101 departments without passenger train service, an airport and a highway. Instead, its historic Train de l'Ardèche tourist steam train, centuries-old vineyards, medieval villages and ancient caves evoke a sense of France as it once was.⁣
 ⁣
Swipe to virtually explore one of France's most rural corners, then tap the link in bio to learn what else the Ardèche has in store.⁣
 ⁣
📷: Erin Henk, Getty Images

Meet the 98-year-old "Angel of Route 66".⁣
 ⁣
Angel Delgadillo still welcomes travellers to Seligman, Arizona – the town he fought to save when the "Mother Road" was forgotten. In 2026, Route 66 will celebrate its 100th anniversary, but without the spark Delgadillo kindled, a centennial celebration wouldn't have happened at all.⁣
 ⁣
Route 66 was established in 1926 as part of the US's first federal highway system, connecting Chicago to Los Angeles. Stretching more than 3,940km (2,448 miles) and passing through Illinois, Missouri, Oklahoma, Texas, New Mexico, Arizona and California, it became a defining American road trip. Today, it is a pilgrimage for domestic and international travellers who come to celebrate the freedom of the open road and classic Americana. Thanks to Delgadillo, it's the US's most mythologised highway. ⁣
 ⁣
Tap the link in bio to learn about the roots of Route 66.⁣
 ⁣
📷: Johnny Kompar

Known by many as Esteban de Dorantes, Esteban the Moor or – most commonly – Estevanico, this individual was one of the first documented Africans to step foot in what is now the United States, arriving nearly 40 years before the first European settlement. ⁣
 ⁣
Between 1528 and 1536, he completed what is widely believed to be the first recorded crossing of North America in history, predating Lewis and Clark's overland expedition to the Oregon Coast by nearly 300 years.⁣
 ⁣
Watch to the end to learn more about the explorer's legacy.⁣
 ⁣
📷: Serenity Strull, Getty Images, Travel Texas

Every year, hundreds of thousands of people descend on Wimbledon expecting world-class tennis. What many first-time visitors don't realise is that they're also signing up for an immersive introduction to British etiquette.⁣
 ⁣
We assembled our top four rules to keep in mind – are they any you would add to the list?⁣
 ⁣
📷: Getty Images

Founded by former Olympic decathlete Tom Waddell in 1982, the Gay Games take place in a different city every four years, and essentially serve as the Olympics for LGBTQ+ athletes.⁣
 ⁣
This summer, the Gay Games will be hosted in Valencia, one of Europe's most queer-friendly cities. The competition will kick off on 27 June, the day of the opening ceremony, with a public 3km (1.68 mile) International Rainbow Memorial Run through the Jardín del Túria.⁣
 ⁣
Swipe to learn more about Valencia as an LGBTQ+ hub, then tap the link in bio for a list of queer-owned and queer-friendly places to visit in the city.⁣
⁣
📷: Visit Valencia, Alamy

With Europe's heatwave season falling early this year, more travellers are embracing an emerging trend: noctourism. ⁣
 ⁣
The traditional, revered European summer holiday has become not just uncomfortable but potentially unsafe – Italy has already issued its first red alert warning people to stay out of the Sun, while both France and Portugal have seen new highest temperature records set. The upcoming summer months promise to be hotter yet.⁣
 ⁣
From midday siestas to more shade in public areas, tap the link in bio for how Europe plans to cool down its most-visited cities and how travellers can adjust for an enjoyable trip.⁣
 ⁣
📷: Alamy, Getty Images

“We are all delighted that we decided to embark on this unforgettable football journey together.”
 
While record-setting ticket prices shut many football fans out of this year’s tournament, joy was the overwhelming sentiment from many who attended the 2026 FIFA World Cup so far.
 
We asked four groups who made the trip what their experiences were like, including how much they spent to attend, who they travelled with, and their impressions of the United States. 
 
Swipe to see some of their reactions, then tap the link in bio for the full story.
 
📷: Serenity Strull, Tomislav Špoljarić, Michael LoRé, John Farley, Nathaniel Grundmann

 ⁣

28 May - Müstəqillik Günüdür 🇦🇿
Bu tarix, 1918-ci il 28 mayda Azərbaycan Xalq Cümhuriyyətinin yaradılması və İstiqlal Bəyannaməsinin qəbulu ilə bağlıdır. Həmin gün Azərbaycan müsəlman Şərqində ilk parlamentli demokratik respublika elan edilmişdi.
Azərbaycan Xalq Cümhuriyyətinin yaradılması və Azərbaycanın müstəqillik tarixində müstəsna rolu olan ən önəmli şəxslər bunlardır:
Məmməd Əmin Rəsulzadə - Azərbaycanın istiqlal ideyasının əsas siması hesab olunur.“Bir kərə yüksələn bayraq, bir daha enməz” sözü ilə tanınır. Milli Şuranın sədri olmuş və istiqlalın elanında aparıcı rol oynayıb.
Fətəli Xan Xoyski - 1918-ci ildə ilk hökuməti formalaşdırıb. Dövlət idarəçiliyinin və hüquqi əsasların qurulmasında böyük xidmət göstərib.
Əlimərdan bəy Topçubaşov - Azərbaycanın beynəlxalq səviyyədə tanınması üçün çalışıb. Paris Sülh Konfransında Azərbaycan nümayəndə heyətinə rəhbərlik edib.
Nəsib bəy Yusifbəyli - Təhsil, dövlət idarəçiliyi və parlament fəaliyyətində mühüm rol oynayıb.
Həsən bəy Ağayev - Parlamentin rəhbərlərindən biri olub və siyasi təşkilatlanmada iştirak edib.
Səməd bəy Mehmandarov və Əliağa Şıxlinski -  milli ordunun yaradılmasına rəhbərlik ediblər.

#28may #retroazerbaijan #musteqillikgunu

Dubai has been named the world’s most beautiful city at night for 2026! 🌃✨ 

According to a 2026 study by JB.com, Dubai claimed the No. 1 spot for its after-dark appeal, ranking ahead of cities like Tokyo and Rome.

The study evaluated destinations based on several factors, including nighttime safety, scenic viewpoints, social media popularity, light pollution, sky brightness, and the variety of nighttime experiences available to visitors.

Dubai’s iconic skyline played a major role in securing the top ranking. From the illuminated Burj Khalifa and the spectacular Dubai Fountain to the glittering skyline of Dubai Marina, Palm Jumeirah, and the city’s vibrant nightlife, Dubai continues to redefine what a city looks like after sunset.

Beyond its famous landmarks, the city offers everything from rooftop lounges and waterfront dining to desert night experiences and late-night shopping, making it one of the world’s most dynamic destinations once the sun goes down.

Whether you’re admiring the skyline from an observation deck, cruising along Dubai Marina, or watching the city lights reflect off the Arabian Gulf, it’s easy to see why Dubai stood out in this global ranking.

Dubai has officially opened its first dedicated women-only beach, offering a new private space designed exclusively for women to relax, swim, and enjoy the coastline! 🏖️✨

Located at Al Mamzar Beach, the new women-only section is part of Dubai’s major beachfront redevelopment project. The area features privacy fencing, female lifeguards and staff, strict no-photography rules, and even dedicated facilities for night swimming.

The beach is designed to provide a comfortable and secure environment while remaining part of the wider Al Mamzar waterfront destination, which also includes walking paths, sports facilities, family areas, and water activities.

For women looking for a more private beach experience, this new addition offers an alternative to traditional public beaches while supporting Dubai’s goal of creating more inclusive and accessible recreational spaces.

Dubai Mall has updated its summer dress guidelines, encouraging visitors to dress respectfully while enjoying one of the world’s most visited shopping destinations! 🇦🇪👕

As Dubai welcomes millions of tourists during the busy summer season, the mall is reminding guests to wear modest clothing in public spaces as a way of respecting the UAE’s local customs and cultural traditions.

The guidance encourages clothing that is appropriate for a family-friendly environment, helping create a comfortable and welcoming experience for both visitors and residents.

The reminder has been widely welcomed by many members of the community, who say it reflects the UAE’s cultural identity and values while promoting mutual respect between people from different backgrounds. Supporters believe the guidance helps preserve local traditions and encourages visitors to be mindful of local etiquette without affecting Dubai’s reputation as an open and welcoming destination.

While Dubai is known for its diverse and international atmosphere, visitors are generally encouraged to dress more modestly in shopping malls, traditional neighborhoods, places of worship, and government buildings. Beachwear is best reserved for beaches and hotel pools.

✨ Understanding local customs is one of the easiest ways to enjoy a smooth and respectful visit, and a small gesture that helps make everyone’s experience more enjoyable.

Dubai is taking another step toward the future of transportation. Residents can now book driverless taxi rides starting from just Dh5 as the city expands its smart mobility network! 🚖🤖 

The new autonomous taxi service is being rolled out by Dubai’s Roads and Transport Authority (RTA) in partnership with Apollo Go, the autonomous ride-hailing platform developed by Chinese tech company Baidu. The service is currently available in Umm Suqeim and Jumeirah, with plans to support Dubai’s long-term vision of making autonomous transport a key part of everyday travel.

The self-driving vehicles rely on a combination of artificial intelligence, high-definition digital maps, cameras, radar, LiDAR sensors, and deep learning technology to detect their surroundings, navigate traffic, and make real-time driving decisions. The goal is to provide a safe, efficient, and convenient travel experience while reducing congestion and improving road safety.

Booking a ride is simple: download the Apollo Go app, choose your pickup and drop-off points within the service area, and use the one-time password (OTP) provided in the app to unlock the vehicle before your journey begins.

As Dubai continues investing in next-generation transport solutions, driverless taxis are expected to play an increasingly important role in the city’s vision of becoming one of the world’s smartest and most innovative cities.
